stories.headline: Atlanta backs up the money wagon for Kyle Pitts: three years, $54 million
stories.read: The Falcons just handed Kyle Pitts the fattest three-year haul a tight end's ever seen, $54 million with $36 million guaranteed, right after the big fella finally gushed: 88 catches, 928 yards, and an All-Pro nod. That kind of money means one thing, pardner: they aim to keep handin' him the reins in '26. Tight end's a barren stretch of prairie most years, and if the saloon's still pricin' Pitts on them lean early seasons, that's cheap gold sittin' in plain sight. Stake that claim before the herd catches up.

stories.headline: Trouble at the Miami corral: Ewers is crowdin' the $67 million man
stories.read: Miami paid Malik Willis $67.5 million to run the outfit, but young Quinn Ewers spent all spring makin' the coaches look twice, splittin' them June practices near down the middle. It's still Willis' saddle to lose, and them runnin' legs make him a fine cheap horse late in your land grab if he keeps it. But a quarterback fight muddies the creek for every Miami pass-catcher, so don't overpay for Jaylen Waddle 'til the dust settles. Watch this corral come camp, pardner; it'll move draft boards.

stories.headline: Burrow's steppin' up under center, and Cincinnati's fixin' to strike a deeper vein
stories.read: Word off the wire says Joe Burrow spent the offseason drillin' snaps from under center after the Bengals ran 'em less than near every outfit in the league. That's where the ol' fake-handoff swindle lives, 'play action' as the city analysts call it, and it's where the long-ball gold hides. More of it means more deep shots rainin' down on Ja'Marr Chase and Tee Higgins, and it raises how high the river can flood for the whole Cincinnati bunch. Pay the toll and ride 'em.
